feature.name: Clone into all descendant selectedcontent elements

intake.finding: ChromeStatus entry 5863985044914176 classifies the work as a PSA / 'No developer-visible change' scoped to two <selectedcontent> edge cases (1) keeping multiple descendant selectedcontent elements up to date rather than only the first in DOM order, and (2) deferring selectedcontent updates that would run during insertion/removal/moving steps to fix security issues, via post-insertion steps or microtasks. Owned by jarhar@chromium.org under Blink>Forms>Select, with spec PR whatwg/html#12263 and tracking bug 458113204 linked. The run manifest records a single planned launch event (M150 Enabled by default). Scope, ownership and evidence links are clear and internally consistent. [chromestatus-5863985044914176][chromestatuslite-5863985044914176][manifest-feature]

incubation.finding: Incubation for this spec-alignment fix is the WHATWG HTML spec thread: PR #12263 'Clone into all descendant selectedcontent elements' opened 2026-03-15, with the ChromeStatus note that Apple/WebKit feedback is the primary motivation. Related open WHATWG issues (#11880 clearing non-primary selectedcontent is broken; #11883 form.reset() does not reset selectedcontent; #12509 updating selected option content does not clone; #11766 changes to option descendants) show continuing spec-defect activity around selectedcontent. Apple is concurrently implementing the parent customizable-select feature in Safari 27 beta (announced WWDC26). [chromestatus-5863985044914176][whatwg-pr-12263][whatwg-issues-selectedcontent][webkit-safari27-customselect]
incubation.limitations: PR #12263 diff could not be retrieved (web reader network errors); no broad developer use-case validation exists, which is appropriate for an edge-case fix.

prototype.finding: Implementation is tracked in bug 458113204 (P3/S2, Blink>Forms>Select, assigned jarhar), explicitly filed on 2025-11-06 because 'test coverage is not sufficient and the spec does not match the implementation', referencing WPT PR 55849. Multiple Chromium CLs landed (7125591, 7127725, 7128135, +10 more; one pending 7922888). The security hardening (deferring updates during insertion/removal/moving steps) is the core prototype work. [crbug-458113204][chromestatus-5863985044914176]
prototype.limitations: Individual CL diffs are not publicly readable; no public regression-test outcomes or telemetry.

developerTrials.finding: No developer trial: this is a PSA 'No developer-visible change' that only affects invalid/edge cases on a feature with ~0.02% usage; there is no developer-facing API surface to trial. [chromestatus-5863985044914176][chromestatuslite-5863985044914176]
developerTrials.limitations: Marked not-relevant because there is genuinely nothing to trial, not because trials were untested/skipped negligently.

wideReview.finding: Tag review marked 'Not applicable' (appropriate for a spec-alignment PSA) and interop risk assessed low. Other-engine engagement is the stated motivation: Apple/WebKit feedback drives the change and Safari 27 beta implements the parent customizable-select feature. Firefox and Web-develop signals are both 'No signal'. [chromestatus-5863985044914176][chromestatuslite-5863985044914176][webkit-safari27-customselect]
wideReview.limitations: Security review and privacy review are both 'Pending' in the cached snapshot and were not re-verified; spec PR #12263 merge state unconfirmed; Firefox has no recorded position.

experiment.finding: No Origin Trial: a PSA with no field uncertainty requiring real-origin experimentation, and rollout_plan is 'Will ship enabled for all users'. [chromestatus-5863985044914176]
experiment.limitations: Marked not-relevant because no field hypothesis needs testing, not because experimentation was avoided while needed.

prepareToShip.finding: Ship plan targets M150 desktop/android/webview (not iOS), rollout 'Will ship enabled for all users', behind finch 'SelectedcontentSpec'; the tracking bug carries Merged-150 and Merged-7871 (the M150 branch) labels, indicating the code is merged to the release branch. [chromestatus-5863985044914176][crbug-458113204]
prepareToShip.limitations: At evidence cutoff ChromeStatus still shows status 'Proposed', is_released=false, security/privacy review Pending, and release-notes not review-ready or publish-ready; finch enablement in stable is unverified.

release.finding: Chrome 150 reached stable on 2026-06-30 (desktop Win/Mac/Linux; 150.0.7871.x in mid-July) and the tracking bug is merged to the M150 branch. However the canonical ChromeStatus record still reads 'Proposed'/is_released=false at cutoff and no release note or PSA post mentions this change, so actual enable-by-default enablement of finch 'SelectedcontentSpec' cannot be confirmed from public evidence. [chromereleases-m150][crbug-458113204][chromestatuslite-5863985044914176]
release.limitations: Cannot verify the finch is enabled-by-default in stable; no release-notes or PSA record found.

adoption.finding: A behavior/spec-alignment PSA with no new capability to adopt; the parent customizable-select feature remains at ~0.02% usage (per ChromeStatus) and <selectedcontent> is not Baseline. Its adoption is tracked separately under the parent feature. [chromestatus-5863985044914176][mdn-selectedcontent]
adoption.limitations: Marked not-relevant because adoption is not a meaningful metric for a no-developer-visible-change fix, not because adoption was unmeasured.

support.finding: No support issues, regression reports, or developer friction attributable to this change were found in public sources.
support.limitations: Too early and too narrow (edge cases on a ~0.02%-usage feature) for a support signal; none located.

deprecation.finding: Not a deprecation or removal; a spec-alignment/security fix (feature_type 'No developer-visible change'). [chromestatus-5863985044914176]
deprecation.limitations: Marked not-relevant because the lifecycle type is not deprecation.

developerValue.finding: Delivers narrow but real value as a correctness/security fix (deferring selectedcontent updates during insertion/removal/moving steps to close security issues) and interop alignment with the WHATWG spec and Apple's direction. Direct developer-facing value is minimal by design ('No developer-visible change') and the affected surface has ~0.02% usage. [chromestatus-5863985044914176][crbug-458113204][mdn-selectedcontent]
developerValue.limitations: No developer demand or satisfaction evidence exists (none is expected for a PSA).

endUserImpact.finding: The only plausible end-user benefit is the security hardening and correctness on edge cases; no quantified end-user impact exists and the affected feature has ~0.02% usage, so success is not inferred. [chromestatus-5863985044914176]
endUserImpact.limitations: Security benefit is unquantified; no end-user telemetry available.

adoption.finding: Adoption is not a meaningful outcome for a no-developer-visible-change spec-alignment fix; there is no new capability to opt into. [chromestatus-5863985044914176]
adoption.limitations: Parent customizable-select adoption is tracked separately.

interoperability.finding: Strong directional alignment: the change is motivated by Apple/WebKit feedback and Apple is implementing the parent customizable-select in Safari 27 beta; WPT coverage is being added (bug cites WPT PR 55849); tag review N/A; interop risk assessed low. Gaps remain: spec PR #12263 merge state is unconfirmed and the WHATWG living-standard text read still describes first-selectedcontent + clear-non-primary semantics; Firefox has no signal; <selectedcontent> is not Baseline. [chromestatus-5863985044914176][crbug-458113204][webkit-safari27-customselect][whatwg-pr-12263][whatwg-html-selectedcontent-spec][mdn-selectedcontent]
interoperability.limitations: Spec/implementation convergence is not externally verifiable; no Safari 27 confirmation of these specific edge-case semantics; PR content not retrievable.

implementationQuality.finding: Active correction of a self-identified gap (bug 458113204: insufficient test coverage + spec/impl mismatch) with multiple landed CLs and explicit security hardening; wpt=true. [crbug-458113204][chromestatus-5863985044914176]
implementationQuality.limitations: CL diffs are not readable; no public regression/telemetry outcomes; the security fix is not independently validated.

evidenceAndCommunication.finding: ChromeStatus PSA communication is honest and well-scoped (PSA because only edge cases + low usage) with clear ownership and accurate signal notes (e.g., Apple feedback is the motivation). However launch-status evidence is incomplete: status still 'Proposed'/is_released=false after M150 shipped, security/privacy reviews Pending in the snapshot, release-notes not review-ready, and no public PSA/release-note record of the behavior/security change. [chromestatus-5863985044914176][chromestatuslite-5863985044914176][chromereleases-m150]
evidenceAndCommunication.limitations: Reviews may be complete outside the cached snapshot; this cannot be confirmed from public evidence.

support.finding: No support signal is available for this change.
support.limitations: Too early and too narrow to assess.

overall.finding: An appropriately scoped, security-motivated spec-alignment PSA with genuine other-engine (Apple/WebKit) engagement and a self-critical bug-driven test/spec effort. However its launch is not verifiably complete at cutoff (ChromeStatus still Proposed, reviews Pending in snapshot, spec PR merge unconfirmed) and measurable outcome evidence is absent — which is expected for a no-developer-visible-change fix and does not by itself imply failure. Overall is mixed because the strongest dimensions (interop direction, implementation correction) are partially offset by incomplete launch-status and spec-convergence evidence. [chromestatus-5863985044914176][crbug-458113204][whatwg-pr-12263][chromestatuslite-5863985044914176][chromereleases-m150]
overall.limitations: Overall does not hide the mixed dimensions; success is not inferred from shipment, the manifest milestone, or absence of criticism.

supports: Feature record: PSA / No developer-visible change; two selectedcontent edge cases incl. security fix; Blink>Forms>Select; owner jarhar; finch SelectedcontentSpec; M150 desktop/android/webview; spec whatwg/html#12263; bug 458113204; ff/safari/webdev No signal; tag review Not applicable; security/privacy review Pending; wpt true; ~0.02% customizable-select usage
sources.limitations: Cached snapshot accurate_as_of 2026-05-29 (pre-M150 ship); is_released=false at snapshot.

supports: Live server-rendered readout still shows Status 'Proposed (Chrome Proposed)' and unchanged signals after M150 shipped, corroborating the launch-status discrepancy
sources.limitations: Mirror of ChromeStatus; confirms current status field but does not prove finch runtime enablement.

supports: Bug 'selectedcontent element behavior does not match spec' filed 2025-11-06 by jarhar; P3/S2; Blink>Forms>Select; states test coverage insufficient and spec does not match implementation; references WPT PR 55849; CLs 7125591/7127725/7128135 (+10 more) and pending 7922888; Merged-150 and Merged-7871 labels
sources.limitations: Comments not fully visible to unauthenticated public; individual CL contents not readable; resolution/verify status not visible.

supports: Spec PR 'Clone into all descendant selectedcontent elements' opened 2026-03-15; closes related selectedcontent issues; is the linked canonical spec for this feature
sources.limitations: Full PR diff/content and merge state could not be retrieved (Z.AI web reader returned persistent network errors for github.com); merge/open state unconfirmed.

supports: Current living-standard text for the selectedcontent element and algorithms: get-enabled returns first selectedcontent descendant; clear-non-primary selectedcontent; clone/clear/update; post-connection and removing steps
sources.limitations: Living standard is undated; the text read still describes first-selectedcontent + clear-non-primary semantics, so whether PR #12263 ('clone into all') is reflected is ambiguous.

supports: <selectedcontent> is 'Limited availability' / not Baseline because it does not work in some widely-used browsers; documents cloneNode-based clone behavior and the dynamic-content caveat
sources.limitations: Reference docs for the element generally; does not describe this specific edge-case/security change.

supports: Chrome 150 to stable channel for Windows, Mac and Linux on 2026-06-30; corroborated by 150.0.7871.x stable versions in mid-July 2026
sources.limitations: Release post does not mention this feature specifically.

supports: Safari 27 beta adds support for customizable <select>; Apple is actively implementing the parent feature, consistent with the ChromeStatus note that Apple feedback motivated this selectedcontent change
sources.limitations: Parent feature; does not confirm Safari 27 implements these specific selectedcontent edge-case semantics.

supports: Apple developer guidance on customizable select (accessibility/progressive-enhancement), confirming ongoing Apple engagement with the feature surface
sources.limitations: Guidance post, not a statement on selectedcontent edge-case semantics.

supports: Parent customizable <select> feature launched from Chrome 135, providing context for the selectedcontent element this change targets
sources.limitations: Parent-feature blog; predates and does not cover this edge-case change.

supports: Cluster of related open WHATWG issues (#11880 clearing non-primary selectedcontent broken; #11883 form.reset does not reset selectedcontent; #12509 dynamic clone; #11766 option-descendant changes) showing continuing spec-defect activity around selectedcontent that PR #12263 addresses
sources.limitations: Related issues, not the content of PR #12263 directly; issue contents only partly retrievable via search snippets.

supports: Single launch event for feature 5863985044914176: eventId '150:Enabled by default:5863985044914176', milestone 150, category 'Enabled by default'
sources.limitations: Planned event derived from the ChromeStatus stage (desktop_first=150); it is not independent corroboration of actual enablement in stable.

successes: Correctly classified and scoped as a PSA / 'No developer-visible change' rather than an Intent to Ship, transparently citing low (~0.02%) usage and edge-case-only impact. [chromestatus-5863985044914176]

Bug-driven with a self-critical tracking issue (458113204) acknowledging insufficient WPT coverage and a spec/implementation mismatch, driving coordinated test + spec work (bug -> whatwg/html#12263). [crbug-458113204][whatwg-pr-12263]

Motivated by and aligned with another engine: Apple/WebKit feedback drives the change and Apple is concurrently implementing the parent customizable-select in Safari 27 beta, supporting interoperability. [chromestatus-5863985044914176][webkit-safari27-customselect]
Security hardening (deferring selectedcontent updates during insertion/removal/moving steps) treated as a first-class part of the change rather than an afterthought. [chromestatus-5863985044914176]
failures: Launch-status tracking is incomplete at cutoff: ChromeStatus status remains 'Proposed' and is_released=false after Chrome 150 stable shipped 2026-06-30, so the manifest's single 'M150 Enabled by default' event is not corroborated by the canonical public record. [chromestatuslite-5863985044914176][chromereleases-m150]

Security and privacy reviews show 'Pending' in the cached snapshot and were not re-verified in public; release-notes were neither content-reviewed nor publish-ready at snapshot. [chromestatus-5863985044914176]

Spec/implementation convergence is not externally verifiable: PR #12263 merge state is unconfirmed and the WHATWG living-standard text read still describes first-selectedcontent + clear-non-primary semantics. [whatwg-pr-12263][whatwg-html-selectedcontent-spec]
No discoverable release-note or PSA record of the behavior/security change exists, and Firefox has no recorded signal on the spec PR. [chromestatus-5863985044914176][whatwg-pr-12263]
counterfactuals: If security/privacy review completion and the finch enablement were recorded on ChromeStatus before/at M150 ship, the launch could be verified as complete rather than inferred from branch-merge labels.

If the spec PR had been merged into the WHATWG living standard (or its merge state surfaced in ChromeStatus), outside observers and other implementers could verify spec/implementation alignment without reading the private bug.
If the owner had flipped ChromeStatus from 'Proposed' to 'Enabled by default' (or recorded the finch rollout state) at the M150 branch, the manifest's launch event would match the canonical record.
A one-line release note or PSA entry would have given developers a discoverable record of the selectedcontent behavior/security change.
If WPT coverage had been sufficient at the original customizable-select ship, the spec/impl mismatch (bug 458113204) might have been caught earlier rather than as a follow-up PSA.
skillImprovements: Add a launch-status reconciliation check that flags manifest 'Enabled by default' events not corroborated by ChromeStatus is_released=true and review-completion at cutoff, surfacing it as a launch-status risk instead of treating the manifest milestone as ground truth.

Add an evidence rule to capture spec-PR merge state (open/merged/closed) plus the living-standard delta for spec-alignment PSAs, so spec/implementation convergence is verifiable without reading private bug trackers.

Add a lightweight 'PSA / no-developer-visible-change' retrospective track that skips developer-trials/experiment/adoption phases but still requires verified finch enablement, review-completion, and a discoverable release-note/PSA record.
Add a regression eval that distinguishes a planned launch event from verified enablement in stable, to prevent inferring success from a manifest milestone alone.
Add an eval case for security-hardening PSAs requiring the security-review status to be resolved (not 'Pending') before the release phase can be marked supported.

missingEvidence: Actual finch 'SelectedcontentSpec' enable-by-default enablement in Chrome 150 stable (ChromeStatus still 'Proposed'/is_released=false at cutoff).
Security and privacy review completion (both 'Pending' in the cached snapshot; not re-verifiable from public sources).
Spec PR whatwg/html#12263 merge state and exact diff (the Z.AI web reader could not retrieve the PR due to persistent network errors).
Independent confirmation that Safari 27 (or Firefox) implements these specific selectedcontent edge-case semantics.
Any release-note or PSA record of the behavior/security change.
